keyMyths.title: The Birth of Zeus
keyMyths.description: Rhea hid Zeus from his father Cronus in a cave on Mount Ida, Crete, to prevent him from being swallowed. Later, Zeus would overthrow Cronus and establish his reign from Mount Olympus.

keyMyths.title: The Titanomachy
keyMyths.description: The Olympian gods, led by Zeus, waged a ten-year war against the Titans from Mount Olympus. This conflict solidified the Olympians' control over the cosmos and their dominion from the mountain.

keyMyths.title: The Council of the Gods
keyMyths.description: Mount Olympus serves as the meeting place for the Olympian gods, where they gather to discuss mortal affairs, settle disputes, and make decisions that impact the world. These councils are often depicted as chaotic and filled with disagreements.

keyMyths.title: Hephaestus' Fall
keyMyths.description: Hephaestus, the god of fire and metalworking, was cast from Mount Olympus by either Hera or Zeus, depending on the account. This event resulted in his lameness and shaped his character as an outsider among the Olympians.

keyMyths.title: Pandora's Creation
keyMyths.description: Zeus, angered by Prometheus' theft of fire, ordered Hephaestus to create Pandora, the first woman. The gods bestowed upon her gifts, but also a jar filled with evils, which she unleashed upon the world after being sent down from Olympus.

sources.name: Hesiod, *Theogony*
sources.description: A foundational text of Greek mythology that outlines the origins of the gods and the cosmos.
sources.title: Hesiod, *Theogony*

sources.name: Homer, *Iliad*
sources.description: An epic poem that depicts the Trojan War and provides insights into the lives and relationships of the Olympian gods.
sources.title: Homer, *Iliad*

sources.name: Apollodorus, *Bibliotheca*
sources.description: A comprehensive handbook of Greek mythology, offering detailed accounts of various myths and legends.
sources.title: Apollodorus, *Bibliotheca*

sources.name: Plato, *Phaedrus*
sources.description: A philosophical dialogue that discusses the nature of love and includes mythological references, including to the gods dwelling on Olympus.
sources.title: Plato, *Phaedrus*

sources.name: Walter Burkert, *Greek Religion*
sources.description: A scholarly work that explores the religious practices and beliefs of ancient Greece.
sources.title: Walter Burkert, *Greek Religion*
